meta.title: Ham Radio General Class — Figure Test
meta.subtitle: Three-question slice for ZIP + figure rendering tests
meta.description: Minimal bank for Q-Primer ZIP and figure-display testing. Contains three G7A questions: a plain question, a figure-referencing question (Figure G7-1), and a plain question — designed to test the transition into and out of figure display.

questions.question: What is the output waveform of an unfiltered full-wave rectifier connected to a resistive load?

answers.A: A series of DC pulses at twice the frequency of the AC input
answers.B: A series of DC pulses at the same frequency as the AC input
answers.C: A sine wave at half the frequency of the AC input

annotation.content: Full-wave rectifier flips both the positive and negative half-cycles of the AC input to the same polarity. Result: 60 Hz AC input produces 120 Hz pulsating DC output (both half-cycles now positive). Each pulse is a half-sine bump. Without a filter capacitor, the output drops to zero between pulses. Contrast with half-wave rectifier: only passes one half-cycle, output frequency equals input frequency.

questions.question: Which symbol in figure G7-1 represents a field effect transistor?

figure.alt: Figure G7-1: Two-stage RF amplifier schematic with numbered component symbols including transistors, diodes, resistors, capacitors, inductors, and a transformer

annotation.content: Figure G7-1 is a two-stage RF amplifier schematic. Symbol 1 is an N-channel JFET. Gate arrow points INTO the channel — inward arrow = N-channel. Symbol 2 (NPN BJT) is a bipolar transistor, not a FET. Symbol 4 is a varactor diode. Symbol 5 is a Zener diode.

questions.question: Which of the following is characteristic of a switchmode power supply as compared to a linear power supply?

answers.A: Faster switching time makes higher output voltage possible
answers.C: High-frequency operation allows the use of smaller components
